mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where unemployment exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which forces equity prices higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio for 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints that choke off revenue growth, causing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods generate positive 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings below 2.0 percent annualized confirm price stability, allowing investors to maintain equity risk premiums and generating positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where annualized CPI growth remains under 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates creates a virtuous employment cycle that boosts corporate profit forecasts and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a negative information coefficient over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product directly reduce aggregate corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production levels above 95.0 indicate robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports near-term earnings revisions and generates positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 weekly returns are negative in over 60 percent of months where the industrial production index exceeds 95.0.
